forecasts.reasoning: Multiple credible sources (including Al Jazeera, Reuters, AFP, and France 24 published on June 4, 2025) report that the Independent Electoral Commission (CEI) of Ivory Coast published the electoral list on June 4, 2025, which confirmed the definitive exclusion of four prominent opposition figures. Former President Laurent Gbagbo is explicitly named as one of the figures excluded from the list due to prior legal convictions. He is one of the candidates explicitly listed in the resolution criteria. The exclusion occurred on June 4, 2025, which is before the July 1, 2025 deadline. Therefore, the conditions for a "Yes" resolution are fully met.

The B.C. government and BC Hydro excluded Tesla products from their EV charger rebate program on March 12, 2025, as part of a retaliatory measure linked to the US-Canada trade disputes (Trump's tariffs). Retaliatory measures of this scale are highly visible and are typically not reversed quickly unless the underlying trade issue is fully resolved. A guide published on July 7, 2025 (Metro EV guide) confirms that the ban is still in effect, stating that Tesla chargers are no longer eligible. Given that the resolution states the page must say they are ineligible on or after July 1, 2025, and we have evidence from July 7, 2025, it is overwhelmingly likely that this rule was indeed in place on the target dates. No evidence of policy reversal was found.

Multiple authoritative sources from October 2025 (The Guardian, WDSU, NPR) report that the 10th and final escapee from the May 2025 New Orleans jailbreak, Derrick Groves, was not captured until early October 2025 in Atlanta. Because at least one inmate was still at large on July 1, 2025, the resolution criteria are not met. Therefore, the question will resolve as No.

The resolution criteria state the question resolves Yes if Lebanon and the IMF announce any economic agreement between the two entities signed before July 1, 2025, involving the IMF agreeing to provide money to Lebanon. Multiple sources and IMF press releases unambiguously indicate that no deal was signed before July 1, 2025. In fact:
1. An IMF mission concluded on June 5, 2025, with a press release stating it was to "initiate discussions on policies and a reform program that could be supported by an IMF arrangement." No agreement was signed here.
2. A subsequent IMF mission visited Lebanon in September 2025 to discuss the reform strategy and the 2026 budget, stating that "engagement is expected to continue."
3. In February 2026, the IMF spokeswoman stated that the IMF "remains engaged in complex discussions with Lebanese authorities following their request for an IMF-supported program in March 2025."
This proves that not only was no deal reached by July 2025, but no deal had been reached by early 2026 either. Thus, this question resolves to No. The probability is set to the lowest allowed bound (0.02).

Donald Trump did not visit Russia between May 19, 2025 and July 1, 2025. Earlier in 2025, he denied French media reports that he would visit Russia in May. In August 2025, Trump and Putin held a highly anticipated summit in Alaska, not Russia. Given the geopolitical context and the fact that their first major meeting occurred in Alaska in August 2025, there is virtually zero chance that he made an unannounced visit to Russia during the narrow May-June window.

The question asks if James Comey will be arrested before July 1, 2025. 
In May 2025, Comey voluntarily met with the Secret Service over a controversial social media post, but was not arrested. Later, in September 2025, he was indicted by a federal grand jury on charges related to his 2020 testimony. The indictment occurred well after the July 1, 2025 deadline. Furthermore, the Wikipedia excerpt confirms that "No arrest warrant was issued" for his September prosecution, and he was arraigned in October under summons. There is no evidence of him being taken into custody or arrested before July 1, 2025. Therefore, the probability of this resolving YES is extremely low.
